channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Élevé] Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
item.description: American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
item.description: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authorities arrested the…

item.title: [Faible] Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live game results on…

item.title: [Faible] OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing
item.description: OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description:
A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what it finds.
The…

item.title: [Faible] OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugging Face's…

item.title: [Critique] LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less than a month after…

item.title: [Faible] Police dismantle Kratos phishing platform, arrest developer
item.description: Authorities in Germany and the U.S. dismantled the central infrastructure of Kratos, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform with global reach, and its developer was arrested in Indonesia. [...]

item.title: [Faible] FakeGit campaign uses 7,600 GitHub repos to push SmartLoader malware
item.description: A large-scale operation dubbed 'FakeGit' is pushing SmartLoader and StealC malware through 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories that accumulated more than 14 million downloads. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Critical SharePoint RCE flaw exploited to steal machine keys
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting the critical CVE-2026-50522 vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint to steal machine keys and maintain access even after affected servers are patched. [...]

item.title: [Faible] DNI nominee Clayton wins Senate panel’s approval
item.description: By a party-line vote, the Senate Intelligence Committee sent the nomination of Jay Clayton to lead ODNI to the Senate floor.

item.title: [Critique] Anubis ransomware claims Coca-Cola Fairlife attack, threatens data leak
item.description: The Anubis ransomware gang has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife dairy subsidiary, threatening to publish allegedly stolen corporate data unless the company pays a ransom. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
item.description:
Apple has moved to address a security flaw in its Hide My Email service that enabled users' real email addresses to be unmasked, effectively undermining the feature's privacy guarantees.
404 Media reported Tuesday that…

item.title: [Faible] Spain fines 23andMe nearly $3 million for cybersecurity failings enabling 2023 hack
item.description: The Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) announced the fine on Friday, saying in its decision that more than 2,600 Spaniards were impacted by a breach affecting 6.9 million people worldwide.

item.title: [Critique] Critical wp2shell WordPress flaws exploited to install webshells
item.description: Hackers are exploiting the "wp2shell" critical vulnerability suite (CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137) affecting WordPress Core to deploy persistent webshells and install malicious plugins on affected servers. [...]

item.title: [Critique] AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
item.description:
Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to stop it.
Intezer, in…

item.title: [Moyen] Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities
item.description: Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch vulnerabilities…

item.title: [Critique] Critical SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-50522 Under Active Exploitation After Public PoC
item.description:
A third SharePoint Server flaw patched by Microsoft as part of its Patch Tuesday update for July 2026 has come under active exploitation, per watchTowr.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-50522 (CVSS score:…

item.title: [Critique] Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments.
Arctic Wolf Labs said it…

item.title: [Élevé] Closing the Identity Gaps in Critical Infrastructure Security
item.description: Critical infrastructure attacks often begin with stolen credentials, compromised devices, or trusted accounts. Specops Software explains why Zero Trust should verify both user identities and device trust before granting…

item.title: [Critique] Taiwan to slow mobile data during national resilience drills
item.description: The speed of 5G and 4G networks across much of Taiwan will be temporarily reduced to 1 percent of capacity as the island holds annual civilian and military exercises.

item.title: [Moyen] Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Zimbra has rolled out fixes to address multiple critical security issues, including a command injection flaw in the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) monitoring component.
As many as nine security…

item.title: [Faible] Kenya probes hack of president's website after bitcoin ransom demand
item.description: The website was hacked on Saturday, when its homepage was replaced with a message displaying a cryptocurrency wallet address and threatening to publish unspecified information about President William Ruto unless the…

item.title: [Critique] Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
item.description: An Android app that can draw over other windows and write to shared storage can slip instructions to the AI agent driving that phone, in text no human eye will ever see. Two more steps, and the same app is running…

item.title: [Moyen] N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.
item.description:
Every patch is a confession.
The moment a vendor ships a security fix, the diff between the old code and the new code tells anyone watching exactly what was broken and where. Turn that diff back into a working exploit,…

item.title: [Faible] New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
item.description:
A cloud tenant using nothing but ordinary GPU access can push a data center's power draw up and down fast enough to threaten the grid it runs on, with no exploit and no break-in.
That is the claim behind Bit2Watt,…

item.title: [Faible] US seizes over 1,000 websites in FIFA World Cup piracy crackdown
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department has seized more than 1,000 websites and blocked 1,970 domains used to stream FIFA World Cup 2026 matches without authorization. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Anubis menace Coca-Cola via Fairlife
item.description: Anubis menace Coca-Cola après une cyberattaque revendiquée contre Fairlife et fixe un ultimatum au 27 juillet.

item.title: [Faible] Coinbase Cartel menace Caterpillar
item.description: Coinbase Cartel menace Caterpillar, expose une stratégie d’extorsion cyber publique et alerte sur des pirates qui usurpent le groupe pirate !

item.title: [Critique] Critical Palo Alto VPN bug now exploited by Qilin ransomware gang
item.description: The Qilin ransomware gang is exploiting a critical PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw to breach victims' networks, according to cybersecurity company Arctic Wolf. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Une usurpation cible les services communication
item.description: Un escroc usurpe Damien Bancal et ZATAZ pour cibler les services communication par ingénierie sociale.
